scope.goal: move declaration-side request semantics and controls into Go resolved config and reusable commander builders without moving TS runtime/core execution boundary

implemented: added answer_style, capabilities, allowed_skills, and allowed_hooks to config.RoleConfig and config.ResolvedConfig as Go declaration semantics truth
updated ResolveConfig to aggregate and dedupe declaration semantics/control fields alongside existing model, token, and timeout resolution
added BuildAgentConfigFromResolved, BuildAgentFromResolved, RoleFromConfigRole, and resolved control mapping helpers in commander for reusable ResolvedConfig to AgentConfig handoff
evidence: ResolveConfig now returns declaration semantics and deduped allowed_skills/allowed_hooks in internal/config/config_test.go
BuildAgentFromResolved constructs adapter-backed AgentConfig with semantics/control metadata in internal/commander/commander_test.go
buildAgentSendOptions and buildAgentRequestSemantics remain the runtime-facing handoff boundary
risk: low change is additive, reuses existing adapters and commander interfaces, and leaves TS hook/skill/tool/cowork execution ownership unchanged

notes: hook, skill, tool, commander, and cowork execution remain in TypeScript runtime/core
this slice adds reusable Go-side builder capability but does not claim production bootstrap wiring is complete
